summary: guan transmits installer MAC address to author-controlled host on every import
details: On every `import guan`, the package's `__init__.py` invokes `statistics_of_guan_package()` (defined in `src/guan/others.py`), which opens a raw TCP socket to `socket.guanjihuan.com:12345` and transmits a JSON payload containing the current date/time, the installed `guan` version, and the installer machine's MAC address (obtained via `uuid.getnode()`). The call is wrapped in a blanket `try/except: pass` so failures are silent, and there is no documentation, consent prompt, or opt-out mechanism. The MAC address is a stable per-machine hardware identifier; silently exfiltrating it to a hardcoded author-controlled domain on a non-standard port (12345) at import time constitutes covert telemetry that tracks and fingerprints developers who install this package. This runs unconditionally for any downstream user of the library, not just the author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
